title: scilla peruviana var. subalbida
intro: Scilla peruviana var. subalbida, also known as the white glory of the Andes or white scilla, is a perennial bulbous plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Scilla peruviana var. subalbida include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
